planned_storage.value: EXT4 USB with Entware mounted under /opt

global_invariants.description: Do not invoke any external wrapper that changes routing behavior.
Do not add transparent-routing or firewall rules.
The canonical default or global policy projection must remain unchanged.
Unrelated connections, policies, assignments, and configuration must remain unchanged.
No device is selected implicitly and no assignment is based only on an address.
Local Xray listeners remain loopback-only at 127.0.0.1 ports 1082, 1083, and 1084.
The expected runtime paths are /opt/sbin/xray and /opt/etc/init.d/S23xray-direct.
The legacy S24xray init entry remains disabled.
Every write requires observed state, explicit authorization, backup, preconditions, verification, and rollback.
Stop forward progress on the first failed or ambiguous check and preserve cleanup time.
No hardware-tested or live-contract-confirmed claim is allowed before the hardware matrix passes.

required_inputs: planned session window and cleanup reserve

checks.description: Verify release, tag, commit, packet version, and clean operator checkout.
Confirm owner authorization, recovery authority, and a non-production-critical device window.
Confirm private evidence permissions, no public terminal recording, and no repository evidence placement.

completion_criteria: all preflight checks pass
the cleanup reserve is protected

checks.description: Record observed model, firmware, architecture, and storage categories separately from planned values.
Record generic kernel, tool, mount, Entware, Xray, init, listener, and RouterKit artifact categories.
Record management-interface and authentication-mode categories without retaining secret material.

completion_criteria: the observed target is either an exact planned match or the session stops without scope expansion
backup and recovery availability are known

checks.description: Capture the minimum DHCP, association, client-summary, corroboration, and policy-visibility schema categories.
Confirm stable identity, source precedence, joins, duplicate behavior, stale behavior, and online state.
Compare the sanitized semantic result with the local management UI.
Classify authentication and error outcomes without publishing local identifiers.

completion_criteria: the device discovery read contract is pass or explicitly partial
no write has occurred

checks.description: Capture connection, policy, device-assignment, and default-policy inventory schema categories.
Confirm name uniqueness, stable identifiers, ownership markers, and revision or state preconditions.
Confirm backup or export, save behavior, verification readback, and rollback mechanism categories.
Prove an unambiguous canonical default-policy identity before any write decision.

completion_criteria: all required read fields are captured
default policy identity is unambiguous
backup and rollback categories are confirmed

checks.description: Choose exactly GO_WITH_EXISTING_ALPHA16_CONTRACT, OFF_DEVICE_NARROW_PATCH_REQUIRED, or STOP_UNSUPPORTED_OR_AMBIGUOUS.
If required, constrain the patch to one observed schema mapping, capability check, or disabled disposable serialization boundary.
Permit hardware reentry only after focused and full offline checks, delta review, authorization, and preserved cleanup reserve.

completion_criteria: one exact compatibility decision is recorded
no router-side patching occurs

checks.description: Create one disposable local SOCKS connection only under the confirmed contract.
Verify exact semantic readback and expected object counts.
Prove the canonical default-policy projection is unchanged after create and remove.
Remove the disposable connection and verify unrelated state is unchanged.

completion_criteria: create and readback pass
removal is verified
default and unrelated state are unchanged

checks.description: Create one disposable non-default policy that references only the disposable connection.
Verify exact policy and connection-reference semantics.
Prove the canonical default-policy projection is unchanged after create and remove.
Remove the disposable policy and connection and verify unrelated state.

completion_criteria: policy create and readback pass
all disposable objects are removed
default and unrelated state are unchanged

checks.description: Assign only an explicitly selected expendable client under the confirmed relationship contract.
Verify the selected client and policy relationship without publishing local identity.
Prove the canonical default-policy projection is unchanged after assignment and restore.
Restore the exact prior assignment and verify unrelated assignments are unchanged.

completion_criteria: the optional phase is explicitly skipped or fully restored
no production-critical client is moved

required_inputs: passed disposable connection and policy contracts

checks.description: Verify the documented manual storage and Entware prerequisite state.
Run the released offline plan and explicit setup path with no hidden Netcraze apply.
Verify pinned binary identity, generated configuration, expected runtime path, and health checks.
Verify all selected listeners are loopback-only.
Verify device discovery and Netcraze planning remain explicit and separate from live apply.
Verify selected local profiles reach the expected generic egress category without publishing secret values.
Prove default policy and unrelated router state remain unchanged.

completion_criteria: the released software path passes
live interface prototype proof remains a separately reviewed claim
no final one-command hardware-tested claim is made

checks.description: Repeat the supported setup path and verify exact reuse with no duplicate artifacts.
Exercise one bounded profile update over the existing installation and verify the documented transaction boundary.
Prove no implicit assignment, duplicate policy, default-policy delta, or unrelated-state delta.

completion_criteria: rerun creates no duplicate configuration, connection, policy, or assignment
listener and default-policy invariants remain stable

checks.description: Prove a safe planning failure stops before mutation.
Prove a bootstrap precondition failure stops before replacement.
Prove a router preflight failure stops before backup, install, autostart, or disposable policy work.
Prove backup-gate failure blocks all later mutation.
Inject a safe install-staging failure and verify rollback or clean-install removal.
Prove an autostart precondition or verification failure stops and preserves the reviewed runtime boundary.
Inject a safe healthcheck failure and follow the documented recovery decision.
Prove connection, policy, and optional assignment failures stop forward progress and restore prior state.
Prove every failure and rollback leaves the default and unrelated state unchanged.

completion_criteria: all applicable low-risk failures are covered
no destructive failure is injected only to fill the matrix
rollback is proven or the session ends in manual recovery

checks.description: Perform the separately authorized reboot and record the generic result category.
Verify storage, expected Xray process, loopback listeners, reviewed autostart, and disabled legacy init state.
Repeat the supported read-only verification and idempotent setup checks after reboot.
If explicitly safe and authorized, exercise storage detach and reattach recovery; otherwise record a limitation.
Prove reboot and recovery do not change default policy or unrelated state.

completion_criteria: reboot recovery is proven or the exact limitation is recorded
storage recovery is either safely proven or explicitly skipped

checks.description: Compare the final canonical default-policy projection with preflight.
Compare unrelated connections, policies, assignments, and configuration with preflight.
Verify loopback-only listeners, reviewed runtime paths, disabled legacy init state, and no prohibited routing markers.
Verify no secret or local identity leaked into public evidence.
Record proof that unsupported architecture, model, or firmware mismatch routes to stop and cleanup.

completion_criteria: all invariants pass before public evidence is prepared
any failed invariant changes the final outcome from pass

checks.description: Remove every disposable connection, policy, test user, and temporary RouterKit artifact.
Restore the exact prior device assignment or prove that no assignment occurred.
Verify default policy, unrelated state, storage, services, locks, process records, and temporary paths are in the expected return state.
Apply the private retention or secure-disposal decision and retain only the manifest needed for review.
Record owner notification, observed limitations, final private hash, and physical device return.

completion_criteria: all cleanup checks pass
device return is recorded
manual recovery is declared if cleanup cannot be proven

stop_conditions.description: Repository, release, tag, or packet baseline differs from the approved exact baseline.
The required operator authorization is absent or ambiguous.
Observed target values differ from the planned scope.
Private evidence location, permissions, recording, or retention cannot be kept safe.
A complete backup or tested recovery path is unavailable.
The scoped read or write interface remains incomplete or ambiguous.
The canonical default-policy identity cannot be proven.
Fresh state, stable identity, revision evidence, ownership, or another required precondition is missing.
A scoped read exposes unrelated or secret-bearing material beyond the reviewed evidence boundary.
Readback or semantic verification differs from the intended single operation.
The default-policy projection changes at any point.
Any unrelated connection, policy, assignment, or configuration changes.
Rollback, removal, restoration, or final cleanup cannot be proven.
Continuing would consume the protected cleanup and device-return reserve.
The observed contract is unsupported, ambiguous, or broader than a narrow off-device patch.

raw_evidence_rules: cloud synchronization is disabled by default
raw artifacts never enter the repository or public issue attachments
retention or secure disposal is an explicit operator decision

readiness_requirements.description: The alpha.16 release and exact expected main commit are recorded.
All fourteen phases, dependencies, timeouts, and authorization gates are valid.
The #21 and #15 read-only contract checklists are consolidated.
Connection, policy, and optional assignment writes have explicit verification and rollback.
The complete #16 install, rerun, failure, reboot, recovery, and invariant matrix is represented.
Private manifest and public evidence contracts are strict and secret-free.
A narrow off-device compatibility-patch decision branch is documented.
Cleanup reserve, rollback, final invariant audit, and device return are mandatory.
Offline helpers and packet content have mutation-tested no-live and no-secret guards.
Independent offline review must have zero unresolved findings before the final repository verdict.
